transformation_type: electrocyclic cyclization of divinyl ketones to cyclopentenones
common_conditions: protic acid or Lewis acid promoted; conrotatory ring closure of pentadienyl cations; nucleophile capture possible
description_short: Electrocyclic ring-closing process that converts divinyl ketones into cyclopentenones under acidic, Lewis acidic, or photochemical conditions.

transformation_type: base-induced rearrangement of O-acyl ketoximes to alpha-amino ketones
common_conditions: O-acyl oxime activation followed by base-induced rearrangement through azirine intermediates
description_short: Method for transforming oximes into alpha-amino ketones via O-acyl activation and alkoxide-promoted rearrangement.

transformation_type: conversion of nitro compounds or nitronates into carbonyl compounds
mechanism_type: acidic or oxidative hydrolysis of nitronate intermediates
common_conditions: acid-dependent hydrolysis or oxidative/reductive variants convert nitro-derived intermediates into carbonyl products
description_short: Classic nitro-to-carbonyl transformation that converts nitronate derivatives into aldehydes or ketones under acidic or related conditions.

transformation_type: Pd- or Ni-catalyzed cross-coupling of organozinc reagents with organic electrophiles
mechanism_type: oxidative addition / organozinc transmetalation / reductive elimination
common_conditions: organozinc reagents couple with broad functional-group tolerance and good stereoretention under Pd or Ni catalysis
description_short: Organozinc-based cross-coupling method valued for high chemoselectivity and compatibility with sensitive functional groups.

transformation_type: annulative synthesis of 5-hydroxyindoles from benzoquinones and enamine partners
mechanism_type: Michael addition / cyclization / redox annulation to substituted hydroxyindoles
common_conditions: benzoquinone/enamine annulation; often gives 5-hydroxyindoles with strong regioselectivity
description_short: Benzoquinone-based indole annulation that furnishes substituted 5-hydroxyindoles from amino-enone or enamine partners.
